title: mammea harmandii
intro: Mammea harmandii, also known as the Thai assegai, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Guttiferae. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Mammea harmandii are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, treat skin infections, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Mammea harmandii is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or dried extracts, which can be consumed as a tea or added to traditional remedies for various health conditions.
